messages.content: In JIRA, I want a JQL request that shows every "test" tickets from a test execution

To show all "test" tickets from a test execution in JIRA, you can use the following JQL (Jira Query Language) request:

```JQL
issueFunction in linkedIssuesOf("project = <your_project_key> AND issuetype = Test Execution", "is caused by")
```

Replace `<your_project_key>` with the actual key of your project, which you can find in the project details or URL.

This JQL query uses the Jira Misc Workflow Extensions (JMWE) add-on, which provides the `linkedIssuesOf()` function. It searches for all issues linked with the "Test Execution" issue type (change it if your issue type is different) using the "is caused by" link type.

By using this query, you will get all the "test" tickets associated with the specified test execution.
I want a JQL request that shows every "test" ticket from a ticket which is a test execution

To show every "test" ticket from a ticket that is a test execution in JIRA, you can use the following JQL request:

```JQL
issueFunction in linkedIssuesOf("issue = <your_test_execution_key>", "is caused by")
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query also uses the `linkedIssuesOf()` function from the Jira Misc Workflow Extensions (JMWE) add-on. It searches for all issues linked with the specified test execution ticket using the "is caused by" link type.

By using this query, you will get all the "test" tickets associated with the specified test execution ticket.
In JQL, show me all associated tickets from a ticket

To show all associated tickets from a specific ticket in JIRA, you can use the following JQL request:

```JQL
issueFunction in connectedIssues(<your_ticket_key>)
```

Replace `<your_ticket_key>` with the actual key of your ticket.

This JQL query uses the `connectedIssues()` function from the Jira Misc Workflow Extensions (JMWE) add-on. It searches for all issues connected to the specified ticket using any type of link.

By using this query, you will get all the associated tickets from the specified ticket.
i want the same thing without extensions and only by using ENhanced Search

To show all associated tickets from a specific ticket in JIRA using Enhanced Search, you can use the following JQL request:

```JQL
issue in linkedIssues(<your_ticket_key>)
```

Replace `<your_ticket_key>` with the actual key of your ticket.

This JQL query uses the `linkedIssues()` function which is built-in to JIRA's Enhanced Search capability. It searches for all issues linked to the specified ticket using any type of link.

By using this query, you will get all the associated tickets from the specified ticket without relying on any extensions.
Show all test tickets from a test execution in JQL, without extensions

To show all "test" tickets from a test execution in JIRA using only JQL (without any extensions), you can use the following JQL request:

```JQL
issueFunction in subtaskOf("<your_test_execution_key>") AND issuetype = Test
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query searches for all issues that are sub-tasks (child issues) of the specified test execution ticket and have the issue type "Test". It will return all the "test" tickets associated with the specified test execution.

By using this query, you can achieve your result without relying on any extensions.
it doesn'(t work

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to find all "test" tickets from a test execution without using any extensions, you can try the following JQL query:

```
issueFunction in parentsOf("project = <your_project_key> AND issuetype = Test AND issueFunction in linkedIssuesOf('issuefunction in inParents(<your_test_execution_key>)', 'is caused by')")
```

Replace `<your_project_key>` with the actual key of your project, and `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query is a bit complex since it's trying to find all the parent "test" tickets of the specified test execution ticket.

By using this query, you can retrieve all "test" tickets associated with the given test execution without any extensions.
still doesn't work

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to show all "test" tickets from a test execution in JIRA without using any extensions, you can try the following JQL query:

```JQL
parent = <your_test_execution_key> AND issuetype = Test
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query searches for all issues whose parent is the specified test execution ticket and have the issue type "Test". It will return all "test" tickets associated with the specified test execution.

By using this query, you should be able to retrieve all "test" tickets associated with the given test execution without using any extensions.
it doesn't show anything

I apologize for the confusion caused. Unfortunately, without using any extensions, it is not possible to directly query for associated tickets from a specific issue in JIRA using JQL.

JIRA's native JQL does not have a built-in function to query for associated tickets. The JQL requests mentioned earlier rely on extensions or plugins to provide additional functionality for querying linked or associated issues.

To achieve your requirement of showing associated "test" tickets from a test execution without using extensions, you may need to consider other approaches such as using JIRA APIs or writing a custom script using JIRA SDK or REST API to fetch the associated tickets programmatically.
what's the test execution field name ?

In JIRA, the field name for a test execution typically depends on the specific testing plugin or add-on you are using. The field name can vary depending on the testing tool or plugin integration.

Some common testing plugins used with JIRA, like Zephyr for JIRA, have their own custom fields for test execution. The field name for a test execution in Zephyr for JIRA is usually "Execution" or "Test Execution".

To find the specific field name for test execution in your JIRA instance, you can follow these steps:

1. Go to the project where the test execution is located.
2. Click on the "Issues" tab.
3. Open one of the test execution issues.
4. Click on the "More" button and then select "Configure".
5. You will be redirected to the "Configure Fields" screen.
6. Look for the field related to test execution in the list, and the field name should be displayed.

If you are unsure about the testing plugin or add-on you are using, you may reach out to your JIRA administrator or the team responsible for managing the testing tools in your organization to get the exact field name for test execution in JIRA.
how to find tests from a test execution by filtering it descending in JQL ?

To find tests from a test execution by filtering it in descending order in JQL, you can use the following JQL query:

```JQL
parent = <your_test_execution_key> AND issuetype = Test ORDER BY created DESC
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query searches for all issues whose parent is the specified test execution ticket and have the issue type "Test". It then orders the results in descending order based on the created date.

By using this query, you should be able to retrieve the tests from the given test execution and sort them in descending order based on the created date.
in JQL , i wanna fetch all the tickets linked to a test execution, how do i do that ?

To fetch all the tickets linked to a test execution in JIRA using JQL, you can use the following JQL query:

```JQL
issueFunction in linkedIssuesOf("key=<your_test_execution_key>")
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query utilizes the `linkedIssuesOf()` function to find all issues linked to the specified test execution ticket. It searches for issues linked by any link type.

By using this query, you will be able to fetch all the tickets linked to the given test execution in JIRA.
what about only "tests" tickets ?

To fetch only "tests" tickets linked to a test execution in JIRA using JQL, you can use the following JQL query:

```JQL
issueFunction in linkedIssuesOf("key=<your_test_execution_key>", "is caused by") AND issuetype = Test
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query utilizes the `linkedIssuesOf()` function in combination with filtering by issue type. It searches for issues linked by the "is caused by" link type, which is commonly used for linking test execution tickets with test tickets. Additionally, it includes a filtering condition to consider only issues with the issue type "Test".

By using this query, you will be able to fetch only the "test" tickets linked to the specified test execution ticket in JIRA.
it doesn't show the tests linked to that test execution

I apologize for the confusion. If you want to fetch only "tests" tickets linked to a test execution in JIRA using JQL, you can use the following JQL query:

```JQL
issue in linkedIssues(<your_test_execution_key>, "is caused by") AND issuetype = Test
```

Replace `<your_test_execution_key>` with the actual key of your test execution ticket.

This JQL query searches for issues directly linked to the specified test execution ticket using the "is caused by" link type. Additionally, it includes a filtering condition to consider only issues with the issue type "Test".

By using this query, you should be able to fetch only the "test" tickets linked to the specified test execution ticket in JIRA.
